prompt: Vertical portrait 9:16 high-resolution atlas world map for a Mutant: Year Zero inspired post-apocalyptic RPG setting. Terrain type: dual_continent. Draw two large ruined-world continents in post-apocalyptic Zone map style, separated by a dramatic non-vertical ocean passage: diagonal and S-shaped irradiated straits, flooded highway causeways, toxic gulfs, broken continental shelves, shoals, quarantine channels, and wreck-strewn narrows rather than a simple straight split. One landmass contains the Ark sanctuary, overgrown city ruins, mutant clan territories, bunker highlands, frozen wastes, and scavenger routes; the other contains irradiated badlands, dead zones, rusted industrial megasites, toxic marshes, old-world military ruins, and hostile survivor enclaves. Complex realistic coastlines on both continents: peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, broken shores, curved coasts, river deltas, fjords, capes, lagoons, barrier islands. Islands are unevenly scattered between and around the continents with varied size and shape, including ruined city-islands, contaminated quarantine isles, reef-like debris fields, and reclaimed green refuge islands. Large detailed survival atlas, grey-green radiation haze palette, hazard symbols and sector grids allowed, no readable labels, no legible text, no logos, no modern satellite style.
